headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative reaction on trust and product criticism, while Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini were in a close tie around neutral-to-mixed sentiment and Anthropic leaned somewhat positive [E1][E4][E5][E6].

ranking.summary: OpenAI skewed negative overall, with accusations of dishonest messaging, complaints about broken behavior and confusing strategy, though there were a couple of positive notes on pricing and a demo [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E7].
Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence and a mildly negative read, mainly from a comment saying Copilot+ was not a convincing selling point; it was in a close tie with Google Gemini [E9].

Google Gemini came out mixed-neutral with some criticism about preview-only releases and being used for basic tasks, but also one comparison that implied it was ahead on context length; it was in a close tie with Microsoft Copilot [E5][E6][E8].
Anthropic leaned positive, with praise for its simpler model lineup and better availability, plus comments preferring Claude for serious work, despite one pricing criticism [E2][E6][E7][E8].

evidence.excerpt: Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly says OpenAI’s messaging was ‘straight up lies,’ which is a strong accusation of dishonesty.

evidence.excerpt: GPT-5.4 ($2.50/M input, $15/M output) is much cheaper than Opus 4.6 ($5/M input, $25/M output)

annotations.rationale: The commenter favors GPT-5.4 on price by saying it is much cheaper.
Opus 4.6 is presented as more expensive in a direct comparison, implying worse pricing.

evidence.excerpt: The "RPG Game" example on the blogpost is one of the most impressive demo's of autonomous engineering I've seen.

annotations.rationale: In the GPT-5.4 story context, calling the demo one of the most impressive seen is strong praise for the showcased OpenAI system.

evidence.excerpt: That's hilarious. Does OpenAI even know this doesn't work?

annotations.rationale: The commenter mocks a broken OpenAI feature and questions whether OpenAI even realizes it, indicating criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Who’s to blame for this ridiculous path they are taking? I’m so glad I am not a Chat user, because this adds so much unnecessary cognitive load. The good news here is the support for 1M context window, finally it has caught up to Gemini.

annotations.rationale: Within a comment explicitly about OpenAI's model lineup, this calls the path ridiculous and complains about cognitive load for users.
Saying OpenAI has finally caught up to Gemini implies Gemini was ahead on this capability.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic are really the only ones who managed to get this under control: Three models, priced at three different levels. New models are immediately available everywhere. Google essentially only has Preview models! The last GA is 2.5. As a developer, I can either use an outdated model or have zero insurances that the model doesn't get discontinued within weeks.

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises Anthropic for having a controlled, simple model lineup and broad availability.
The complaint that Google mostly has preview models and that the last GA is 2.5 criticizes Gemini-family release stability and availability.

evidence.excerpt: There are no ‘good’ companies but I like anthropic little bit more than the others as of this moment right now. Would buy their stock, would sell OpenAI, maaybe.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says they like Anthropic more than the others.
Saying they would sell OpenAI contrasts unfavorably with buying Anthropic.

evidence.excerpt: he uses the corporate approved LLM (Gemini) for “pretend work” or very basic tasks. At the same time he uses Claude on his personal account to seriously augment his job.

annotations.rationale: Gemini is described as suitable only for ‘pretend work’ or very basic tasks.
Claude is described as the tool used to seriously augment real job performance.

evidence.excerpt: > This makes them AMD’s first desktop chips to qualify for Microsoft’s Copilot+ PC label, which enables a handful of unique Windows 11 features like Recall and Click to Do. This is not the selling point they think it is.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly rejects Copilot+ qualification as an appealing selling point.
